source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Two parallel smooth metal rails \( M N \) and \( P Q \) are placed on a horizontal plane, with a spacing \( L = 0.2 \, \mathrm{m} \). A uniform magnetic field with magnetic induction \( B = 5 \, \mathrm{T} \) is directed vertically downward, perpendicular to the plane of the rails. A resistor \( R = 4.8 \, \Omega \) is connected between the two rails. On the rails, there is a metal rod \( a b \) with a resistance \( r = 0.2 \, \Omega \) in the circuit. The metal rod is perpendicular to the rails and makes good contact with them. A horizontal force is applied to the rod \( a b \), causing it to move to the right at a constant speed \( v = 5 \, \mathrm{m/s} \). Assume the metal rails are sufficiently long.

1. What is the induced electromotive force generated by the metal rod $a b$?
2. What is the magnitude and direction of the current through the resistor $R$?
3. What is the magnitude of the horizontal pulling force $F$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
$5\mathrm{V}$
$1\mathrm{A}$, flowing from M through R to P.
$1\mathrm{N}$

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false in the input metadata
Question uses diagram-specific labels and spatial setup: rails MN and PQ, rod ab, and current direction relative to M/P
Current direction and geometric interpretation are easier to ground from the registered image, which exists and passed registry checks
multi_step: The problem is explicitly split into three linked sub-questions: induced emf, circuit current, and pulling force
Solving later parts depends on earlier results: emf -> current through total resistance -> magnetic force / pulling force
Requires combining multiple physics relations and given quantities (B, L, v, R, r) rather than a single lookup
verifiability: Answer is a compact set of concrete outputs: two numeric values with units, one current magnitude plus direction, and one force value
These outputs are checkable by standard formulas for motional emf, series-circuit current, and magnetic force balance at constant speed

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
Two parallel smooth metal rails \( M N \) and \( P Q \) are placed on a horizontal plane, with a spacing \( L = 0.2 \, \mathrm{m} \). A uniform magnetic field with magnetic induction \( B = 5 \, \mathrm{T} \) is directed vertically downward, perpendicular to the plane of the rails. A resistor \( R = 4.8 \, \Omega \) is connected between the two rails. On the rails, there is a metal rod \( a b \) with a resistance \( r = 0.2 \, \Omega \) in the circuit. The metal rod is perpendicular to the rails and makes good contact with them. A horizontal force is applied to the rod \( a b \), causing it to move to the right at a constant speed \( v = 5 \, \mathrm{m/s} \). Assume the metal rails are sufficiently long.

1. What is the induced electromotive force generated by the metal rod $a b$?
2. What is the magnitude and direction of the current through the resistor $R$?
3. What is the magnitude of the horizontal pulling force $F$?
open_ended_problem_variants.expected_answer:
$5\mathrm{V}$
$1\mathrm{A}$, flowing from M through R to P.
$1\mathrm{N}$
